title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) with this easy-to-follow recipe. A healthy, flavorful, and comforting Middle Eastern classic.

seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?
Step-by-Step Instructions for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
Pro Tips for the Best Adas Soup
Serving Suggestions for Red Lentil Soup
Variations and Substitutions for Lebanese Adas
seo.imageAlt: A comforting bowl of authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm, homemade bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a healthy and flavorful Middle Eastern staple.

description: Experience the heartwarming embrace of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas. This traditional Middle Eastern staple is a nourishing blend of red lentils, fresh vegetables, and aromatic spices, simmered to perfection. It's a truly comforting and healthy dish, perfect for any season.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Dice the onion, carrot, and potato into small, uniform pieces. Mince the garlic cloves.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the rinsed red lentils, diced carrot, ground cumin, ground coriander, and turmeric powder. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly to toast the spices and coat the lentils and vegetables.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth and add the diced potato. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend (Optional) and Season
instructions.text: For a smoother consistency, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup, leaving some texture, or blend about half of the soup in a regular blender and return it to the pot. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh lemon juice.

instructions.title: Serve Adas
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro or parsley and serve immediately with extra lemon wedges and a drizzle of good quality olive oil, if desired. It pairs wonderfully with pita bread.

tips: Always rinse your lentils thoroughly to remove any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy.
Adjust the consistency of your soup by adding more hot vegetable broth if you prefer a thinner soup, or simmering longer if you prefer it thicker.
For an extra layer of flavor, finish your soup with a drizzle of good quality extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkle of dried mint just before serving.
article:
A bowl of warm, comforting soup has a unique power to soothe the soul and nourish the body. Among the countless culinary treasures of the Middle East, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known affectionately as Adas, stands out as a true masterpiece. This hearty, wholesome, and incredibly flavorful soup is more than just a meal; it's a staple, a tradition, and a beloved symbol of Lebanese home cooking. Naturally vegan and gluten-free, Adas is a testament to how simple ingredients can come together to create something truly extraordinary. Whether you're seeking comfort on a chilly evening, a nutritious lunch, or an authentic taste of Lebanese cuisine, this vibrant red lentil soup is guaranteed to delight your senses.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas)?

Adas, simply meaning "lentils" in Arabic, refers to a category of dishes, with the red lentil soup being one of the most popular. This particular Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is celebrated for its beautiful reddish-orange hue, creamy texture, and bright, tangy flavor profile. Unlike some lentil soups that might be heavy or overly spiced, Adas offers a refreshing lightness, primarily due to the generous use of fresh lemon juice and dried mint. It's a cornerstone of Lebanese cuisine, often appearing on tables during the colder months, throughout the fasting period of Lent, or simply as a comforting weeknight meal. The simplicity of its ingredients – red lentils, onions, garlic, olive oil, lemon, and mint – belies the depth of flavor it achieves. It’s a truly nourishing dish, packed with plant-based protein, fiber, and essential nutrients, making it a favorite for health-conscious eaters and food lovers alike.

## Ingredients You Need for Adas

Crafting the perfect Lebanese Red Lentil Soup requires a handful of fresh, high-quality ingredients. Here's what you'll need:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Use split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily, contributing to the soup's creamy texture.
*   **Onion:** A medium yellow onion, finely chopped, forms the aromatic base.
*   **Garlic:** Fresh garlic cloves, minced, provide a pungent depth of flavor. Don't skimp on the garlic!
*   **Carrots (Optional but Recommended):** One medium carrot, finely diced, adds a subtle sweetness and extra nutritional value.
*   **Olive Oil:** High-quality extra virgin olive oil is essential for sautéing and drizzling over the finished soup.
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** While water works perfectly fine, vegetable broth will lend an even richer flavor to your soup.
*   **Cumin:** Ground cumin is the primary spice, imparting a warm, earthy aroma that is quintessential to Middle Eastern cooking.
*   **Salt and Black Pepper:** To taste, adjusting as needed throughout the cooking process.
*   **Dried Mint:** This is a non-negotiable ingredient! Dried mint adds a unique, bright, and cooling flavor that defines Lebanese Red Lentil Soup.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice:** Squeezed from fresh lemons, this is added at the end to provide that signature tangy zest and balance the richness.
*   **Optional Garnishes:** Fresh cilantro or parsley, extra lemon wedges, a sprinkle of sumac, or toasted pita bread for dipping.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

Making Adas is straightforward and rewarding. Follow these steps for a delicious bowl:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2-3 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté for 5-7 minutes until softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and diced carrots (if using), and cook for another 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic.
3.  **Add Lentils and Broth:** Stir in the rinsed and drained red lentils. Pour in the vegetable broth or water, ensuring the lentils are fully submerged. Add the ground cumin, salt, and black pepper.
4.  **Simmer:** Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Achieve Desired Consistency:** Once the lentils are cooked, you have a choice. For a chunkier soup, you can leave it as is. For a smoother, creamier soup (which is traditional for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup), use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions to a regular blender (blending in batches) and return to the pot.
6.  **Add Finishing Touches:** Stir in the dried mint and fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasonings – you might need more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to achieve the perfect balance.
7.  **Optional Garlic-Mint Sauté (Tasha):** For an extra layer of flavor, heat 1-2 tablespoons of olive oil in a small pan. Sauté an additional minced garlic clove for 30 seconds until fragrant, then add a pinch more dried mint (about 1/2 teaspoon) and cook for another 15 seconds. Immediately pour this aromatic oil over the finished soup just before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Adas Soup

Elevate your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup with these expert tips:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils:** Don't skip this step! Rinsing removes impurities and helps prevent the soup from becoming cloudy or foamy.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice is Key:** Bottled lemon juice simply won't yield the same bright, vibrant flavor. Always use freshly squeezed lemon juice. Add it at the end to preserve its fresh tang.
*   **Don't Overcook:** While lentils should be tender, overcooking can make them too mushy and lose their slight texture. Keep an eye on them after 20 minutes.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If your soup is too thick, add a little more hot broth or water until it reaches your desired consistency. If it's too thin, let it simmer uncovered for a few more minutes to reduce.
*   **The Dried Mint Factor:** Dried mint is crucial for authentic flavor. While fresh mint can be used as a garnish, dried mint infused into the soup during the final stages creates that distinctive Lebanese taste.
*   **Resting Period:** Like many soups, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup often tastes even better the next day after the flavors have had a chance to meld and deepen.
*   **Quality Olive Oil:** A good quality extra virgin olive oil enhances the overall flavor and mouthfeel, especially when drizzled as a garnish.

## Serving Suggestions for Red Lentil Soup

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. Here are some popular serving suggestions:

*   **With Fresh Lemon Wedges:** Always serve Adas with extra lemon wedges on the side, allowing each person to add more tanginess to their liking.
*   **Crusty Bread or Pita:** A must-have for dipping! Warm pita bread or a fresh, crusty baguette is perfect for soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Drizzle of Olive Oil:** A final swirl of good quality extra virgin olive oil just before serving adds richness and a beautiful sheen.
*   **Fresh Herbs:** Garnish with a sprinkle of fresh chopped cilantro or parsley for added freshness and color.
*   **Spicy Kick:** If you enjoy a little heat, a pinch of red pepper flakes or a drizzle of your favorite hot sauce can be a welcome addition.
*   **Alongside a Salad:** For a complete and balanced meal, serve your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup with a simple side salad, such as a traditional Lebanese Fattoush or a basic cucumber and tomato salad.
*   **As an Appetizer:** Adas can also be served in smaller portions as a warming and flavorful appetizer before a larger meal.

## Variations and Substitutions for Lebanese Adas

While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or dietary needs.

*   **Add More Vegetables:** Feel free to incorporate other finely diced vegetables like celery, potatoes (for extra creaminess), or even a handful of fresh spinach or Swiss chard added during the last 5-10 minutes of cooking.
*   **Spice It Up:** A pinch of turmeric can add a beautiful golden hue and subtle earthy notes. Some recipes include a tiny dash of allspice for a more complex aroma. For heat, a small amount of cayenne pepper or a fresh chili can be added during the sautéing stage.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can introduce a lovely smoky depth.
*   **Broth Alternatives:** While vegetable broth keeps it vegan, you can use chicken or beef broth if you prefer a non-vegetarian version.
*   **Garnish Creativity:** Beyond the traditional, consider fried onions, a swirl of plain yogurt (if not strictly vegan), or even a drizzle of pomegranate molasses for a sweet and sour contrast.
*   **Texture Control:** If you prefer a soup with more texture, blend only half of the soup, leaving the other half chunky. This gives you the best of both worlds.

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is more than just a recipe; it's an experience of wholesome goodness and vibrant flavors. Its simplicity makes it approachable for any home cook, while its deliciousness ensures it will become a regular in your kitchen. Enjoy the warmth, the nutrition, and the authentic taste of the Middle East with every comforting spoonful.
